questions.question: Which gas is required for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants use CO₂ and sunlight to produce glucose during photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which Assamese literary figure wrote 'Miri Jiyori'?
questions.explanation: Rajanikanta Bordoloi wrote the famous Assamese novel 'Miri Jiyori'.

questions.question: The 2025 Assam flood affected approximately how many people?
questions.explanation: Assam floods in 2025 affected about 30 lakh people.

questions.question: What is the voting age in India?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj system was constitutionalised by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj.

questions.question: The Battle of Plassey was fought in which year?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey (1757) marked the beginning of British rule in India.

questions.question: Which organization conducts the Union Public Service Commission exams?
questions.explanation: UPSC conducts civil services examinations for the central government.

questions.question: How many schedules are there in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution originally had 8 schedules, now has 12.

questions.question: What is the capital of Assam?
questions.explanation: Dispur, within the Guwahati metropolitan area, is the capital of Assam.

questions.question: Who was elected as the President of India in the 2025 election?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu was elected President in 2022; if re-elected in 2025, same applies.

questions.question: What is the atomic number of Carbon?
questions.explanation: Carbon has atomic number 6 with 6 protons in its nucleus.

questions.question: Which organisation declared the Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary a 'Important Bird Area'?
questions.explanation: BirdLife International declared Pobitora an IBA in 2004.

questions.question: Who was the first Indian to win a Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Tagore won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913.

questions.question: What is the currency of India?
questions.explanation: The Indian Rupee (₹) is the official currency.

questions.question: Which gas is most abundant in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere.

questions.question: Which national park in Assam is a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: Kaziranga was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1985.

questions.question: The famous Battle of Haldighati was fought between which two forces?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Haldighati (1576) was between Akbar and Maharana Pratap.

questions.question: How many High Courts are there in India (as of 2025)?
questions.explanation: India has 25 High Courts as of 2025.

questions.question: Which Indian space mission was launched to study the Sun?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 is India's first solar mission launched in 2023.

questions.question: Which Indian company became the most valuable company by market cap in 2025?
questions.explanation: TCS has been among the most valuable companies.

questions.explanation: Assam has promoted bamboo products under ODOP.

questions.question: The slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' was given by which leader?
questions.explanation: Shastri gave this slogan during the 1965 India-Pakistan war.

questions.explanation: Assam borders Arunachal Pradesh for about 800 km.

questions.explanation: Saturn has 146 confirmed moons as of 2025.

questions.question: Who was elected as the President of India in the 2025 election?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu was elected President in 2022; if re-elected in 2025, same applies.

questions.explanation: Lord Curzon partitioned Bengal in 1905, later revoked in 1911.

questions.explanation: Lok Sabha is the lower house of Parliament representing the people.

questions.explanation: The Vice President serves a term of 5 years.

questions.question: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be hosted by which countries?
questions.explanation: The 2026 World Cup is jointly hosted by USA, Canada, and Mexico.

questions.explanation: The 2026 World Cup is jointly hosted by USA, Canada, and Mexico.

questions.explanation: Subpoena is not a constitutional writ; 5 writs exist: Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Certiorari, Prohibition, Quo Warranto.

questions.explanation: The Eighth Schedule lists 22 official languages of India.

questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution is known as the 'Mini-Constitution'?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) made sweeping changes to the Constitution.

questions.explanation: Pragyan Bharti is Assam's scheme for higher education students.

questions.explanation: India took over as UNSC president in January 2026.

questions.question: What is the maximum strength of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The maximum strength is 550 members (530 from states + 20 from UTs).

questions.question: Which country hosted the G20 Summit in 2025?
questions.explanation: India hosted the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2025.

questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak founded the Slave (Mamluk) Dynasty in 1206.

questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'Aurum' meaning gold.

questions.question: What is the state animal of Assam?
questions.explanation: The Indian one-horned rhinoceros is the state animal of Assam.

questions.explanation: Light travels at about 299,792,458 m/s in vacuum.

questions.explanation: Shastri gave this slogan during the 1965 India-Pakistan war.

questions.explanation: Sivasagar (formerly Rangpur) was the capital of the Ahom Kingdom.
